docs: add comprehensive release notes for v1.1.0

Documents all major features and improvements delivered:

Major Features:
- Host path preservation (breaking change from /workspace)
- Professional configuration interface with packnplay configure
- Configurable default container with any image support
- Enhanced container information and debugging
- Smart version update notifications

Technical Improvements:
- Safe configuration editing with setting preservation
- Text field editing using bubbles textinput component
- Enhanced error messages with actionable suggestions
- Improved container management and lifecycle

Breaking Changes:
- Container mounting now uses host paths for consistency
- Enhanced command interfaces and improved UX

This provides complete documentation for users upgrading
and highlights the significant improvements delivered.

feat: add visual progress bars for Docker image downloads and builds

## Progress Bar System
- Real-time progress bars for docker pull and docker build operations
- JSON progress parsing for precise download percentages and byte counts
- Plain text parsing for Docker build status updates
- Throttled updates (100ms) to prevent terminal flooding

## Visual Features
- Styled progress bars using lipgloss for consistency
- Success (✅) and error (❌) completion indicators with timing
- Byte-formatted progress details (MB/GB) for downloads
- Automatic terminal detection (only shows in interactive mode)

## Smart Output Handling
- docker pull: reads JSON progress from stdout
- docker build: reads plain text progress from stderr
- Verbose mode: bypasses progress bars, shows raw Docker output
- Graceful fallback when progress parsing fails

## Integration Points
- Enhanced Docker client with RunWithProgress() method
- Updated ensureImage() in runner to use progress for pull/build
- Comprehensive test coverage for progress tracking and rendering
- Zero impact on existing functionality or error handling

Improves user experience during container setup by providing clear
feedback on potentially long-running download and build operations.

fix: eliminate container cleanup timeout warnings in E2E tests

Problem:
- E2E tests consistently showed "context deadline exceeded" warnings
  during container cleanup
- cleanupContainer used docker stop + docker rm -f approach
- docker stop waits up to 10s (default timeout) for SIGTERM response
- Containers running "sleep infinity" always take full 10s to stop
- This caused slow cleanup and frequent timeout warnings

Solution:
- Replace docker stop + docker rm with single docker rm -f command
- docker rm -f immediately kills (SIGKILL) and removes in one operation
- Reduced timeout from 30s to 10s (cleanup is now much faster)
- Added check to suppress "No such container" errors (expected case)

Performance Impact:
- Old approach: ~10+ seconds per container
- New approach: ~0.16 seconds per container
- 63x faster cleanup in real-world testing

This is appropriate for test cleanup where graceful container shutdown
is not required. Production code should still use graceful shutdown.

for mount paths, but was not being passed to docker run or docker exec
commands. This caused containers to always run as root regardless of

- Add --user flag to docker exec in both new container and reconnect code paths
- Add postStart command execution to reconnect path for consistency

- Docker resolves to: /Users/user/Documents/GitHub/project

- Ensures consistent path resolution between container creation and reconnection
- Handles symlink chains and complex directory structures

fix: add LoadLockFile tests and ensure consistent lockfile usage

**Unit Tests:**
Add comprehensive unit tests for LoadLockFile function covering:
- Valid lockfile with multiple features
- Missing lockfile (returns nil, nil)
- Invalid JSON (returns error)
- Empty features map

**Lockfile Consistency:**
Load lockfile once in runner.Run() and pass to all three feature resolution points:
1. Image build (via ImageManager.EnsureAvailableWithLockfile)
2. Property resolution (line 983 in runner.go)
3. Lifecycle merging (line 1132 in runner.go)

Previously, lockfile was only loaded in image_manager.go for image builds,
causing inconsistency where property/lifecycle resolution used latest versions
instead of locked versions.

Changes:
- Load lockfile in runner.Run() after loading devcontainer config
- Add EnsureAvailableWithLockfile method to ImageManager
- Add buildImageWithLockfile and buildWithFeaturesAndLockfile methods
- Pass lockfile to feature resolvers in all three resolution points
- Maintain backward compatibility with deprecated methods

Fixes important issues from code review ensuring image build, property
resolution, and lifecycle merging all use the same locked feature versions.

feat: implement devcontainer lockfile support (devcontainer-lock.json)

Add support for devcontainer-lock.json to pin feature versions for reproducible builds.
This implements Task 6 from the full devcontainer spec compliance plan.

Changes:
- Add LockedFeature and LockFile structs to config.go
- Add LoadLockFile() function to load devcontainer-lock.json
- Update FeatureResolver to accept and use lockfile for version pinning
- Update image_manager.go to load and pass lockfile to resolver
- Update all NewFeatureResolver() calls to include lockfile parameter
- Add E2E test to verify lockfile support

The lockfile format follows Microsoft's specification:
{
  "features": {
    "ghcr.io/devcontainers/features/node:1": {
      "version": "1.2.0",
      "resolved": "ghcr.io/devcontainers/features/node:1.2.0"
    }
  }
}

When a lockfile exists, the resolver will use the locked version instead of
pulling the latest version, ensuring reproducible builds across environments.

security: fix critical vulnerabilities in HTTPS tarball feature download

Addressed multiple security issues in the downloadHTTPSFeature function:

1. HTTP Client Timeout (CRITICAL)
   - Added 30-second timeout to HTTP client to prevent indefinite hangs
   - Previously used default http.Get() with no timeout

2. Request Size Limit (CRITICAL)
   - Implemented 100MB maximum size limit using io.LimitReader
   - Prevents disk/memory exhaustion from unbounded downloads
   - Returns clear error when size limit exceeded

3. Content-Type Validation (IMPORTANT)
   - Added validation for expected tarball content types
   - Accepts: application/x-gzip, application/gzip, application/x-tar,
     application/x-compressed-tar, application/octet-stream
   - Rejects invalid content types to prevent malicious files

4. Tarball Path Traversal Protection (IMPORTANT)
   - Ensured tar extraction doesn't use -P flag
   - Relies on tar's default behavior of stripping absolute paths
   - Added comment documenting security rationale

5. Test Infrastructure Improvement (IMPORTANT)
   - Replaced external server dependency with httptest.Server
   - Test now creates tarball programmatically for self-contained testing
   - Eliminates flaky tests from external dependencies

All tests pass. No functional changes to user-facing behavior.
